issue_title: Decrypt fails (IncompleteSignatureException) depending on path, cwd
issue_body:
This is on a Mac, I was unable to reproduce this on my Linux machine.

The encrypted file is in, for example, `/Users/myself/some/random/path`.

If calling `sops -d /Users/myself/some/random/path/file.enc.json`, the following error occurs:

```
Group 0: FAILED
  arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:831617005271:key/9f8c0d27-f319-42bd-9e54-e20f59b44ae3: FAILED
    - | Error decrypting key: IncompleteSignatureException: 'access'
      | not a valid key=value pair (missing equal-sign) in
      | Authorization header: 'AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=paste
      | access key/20180117/us-east-1/kms/aws4_request,
      | SignedHeaders=content-length;content-type;host;x-amz-date;x-amz-target,
      | Signature=e56c097f7ec0554816b86dff449a0c3b3b6d2b3867d73c3799f08393938255a9'.
      | 	status code: 400, request id:
      | 284ea281-fbaf-11e7-ad69-3f88f6957d83
Recovery failed because no master key was able to decrypt the file. In
order for SOPS to recover the file, at least one key has to be successful,
but none were.

```

From `/Users/myself/another_dir`, if running `sops -d ../some/random/path/file.enc.json`, the error also occurs.

Only case we can get it to work is by cd'ing into `/Users/myself/some/random/path` and running `sops -d file.enc.json`.

File is encrypted with a KMS key and confirmed the user can access the key.

issue_title: Outputting value from YAML file to stdout has trailing ellipsis
issue_body:
We are using SOPS to dump specific values in some scripts. Unfortunately, SOPS seems to be adding a `...` to the end of the output. Here are steps to reproduce:

``` bash
$ # Generate a temporary PGP backed file
$ sops tmp.yml --pgp 'XXXX'
INFO: tmp.yml doesn't exist, creating it.
INFO: generating and storing data encryption key
INFO: temp file created at /tmp/tmpJC0MRr.yaml
INFO: file written to tmp.yml
$ # Output content in plaintext
$ sops tmp.yml --decrypt
gpg: encrypted with 2048-bit RSA key, ID XXXX, created 2016-09-09
      "XXXX"
foo: bar
$ # Output value on leaf of YAML tree
$ sops tmp.yml --decrypt --extract '["foo"]'
gpg: encrypted with 2048-bit RSA key, ID XXXX, created 2016-09-09
      "XXXX"
bar
...
```

After doing some digging, it looks like this is due to us doing an `fd` check along side `isinstance(tree, list)/isinstance(tree, dict)` when we shouldn't. As a result, we are dumping a value as YAML when it's not:

https://github.com/mozilla/sops/blob/1.14/sops/__init__.py#L1281-L1285

https://github.com/mozilla/sops/blob/1.14/sops/__init__.py#L1287-L1292

Is there a way that I can get around or I can put the file on somewhere else where I do not to get extra permission? Thx a lot!

https://www.djangoproject.com/download/#supported-versions

Django 1.10 is no longer supported, so we should drop support for it, too.

When an HTTPException is raised, and [render_error()](https://github.com/quantmind/pulsar/blob/74d1e6e58095679291c14c826ce63f6312a93256/pulsar/apps/wsgi/utils.py#L262) decide to return HTML, the exception message should be escaped to prevent [XSS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cross-site_scripting).

[OWASP recommends it](https://www.owasp.org/index.php/XSS_(Cross_Site_Scripting)_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet#RULE_.231_-_HTML_Escape_Before_Inserting_Untrusted_Data_into_HTML_Element_Content) and [Werkzeug does it.](https://github.com/mitsuhiko/werkzeug/blob/d9e7736de41e7cbf5de70a15e28449b4dd4adab0/werkzeug/exceptions.py#L113)

issue_title: #1607 Regression - self.clone casts parameters to destination type
issue_body:
So #1607 has broken one of my design patterns - it could be my bad choice of design pattern (yay, big refactor!) or it could be something to address here.

The `self.clone` method, which we use to pass parameters a set of parameters from one task to another, casts source parameters to their destination parameters type.

As there's no abstract `Parameter` class, and Parameter is effectively a string, when you try and clone into a task with `luigi.Parameter` it, it will always cast to string.

```python
import luigi

class HLITargetTask(luigi.Task):
    authors = luigi.Parameter()
    def complete(self):
        return True

class HLISourceTask(luigi.Task):
    authors = luigi.DictParameter({'original': 'Jason', 'refactor': 'James'})
    def requires(self):
        return self.clone(HLITargetTask)
    def complete(self):
        return False

t = HLISourceTask()

luigi.build([t], local_scheduler=True)
```

```bash
/Users/jpiper/envs/annotation/lib/python2.7/site-packages/luigi/parameter.py:234: UserWarning: Parameter <FrozenOrderedDict OrderedDict([('original', 'Jason'), ('refactor', 'James')])> is not of type string.
  warnings.warn("Parameter {0} is not of type string.".format(str(x)))

===== Luigi Execution Summary =====

Scheduled 2 tasks of which:
* 1 present dependencies were encountered:
    - 1 HLITargetTask(authors=<FrozenOrderedDict OrderedDict([('original', 'Jason'), ('refactor', 'James')])>)
* 1 ran successfully:
    - 1 HLISourceTask(authors={"original": "Jason", "refactor": "James"})

This progress looks :) because there were no failed tasks or missing external dependencies

issue_title: Boto3 1.4.1 client `describe_instances` API returns null result in case there are more than 10 tags specified in the `Filters`
issue_body:
It seems that the [issue with > 10 tags in the filters](https://github.com/boto/boto/issues/3637) that we previously observed working with Boto 2 isn't inherent to Boto 2, as we have since replicated the issue with Boto 3 as well:

In case we use more than 10 tag/value pairs in the call to `describe_instances` from [`client.describe_instances`](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/ec2.html#EC2.Client.describe_instances) we get an empty set instead of the list of instances. Below is an example:

	>>> from aws_common3 import get_ec2_instances
	>>> args = dotdict()
	>>> args.tags = "Availability:test-schedule Backup: CostCentre: CreatedBy: Division: Environment:Prod ManagedBy: Name:test-snapshots-2vols Owner: Tag10:"
	>>> tags = [ { t.split(":")[0] : t.split(":")[1] } for t in args.tags.split(" ") ]
	>>> tags
	[{'Availability': 'test-schedule'}, {'Backup': ''}, {'CostCentre': ''}, {'CreatedBy': ''}, {'Division': ''}, {'Environment': 'Prod'}, {'ManagedBy': ''}, {'Name': 'test-snapshots-2vols'}, {'Owner': ''}, {'Tag10': ''}]
	>>> filters = [ { "Name": "tag:"+t.keys()[0], "Values": [ t.values()[0] ] } for t in tags ]
	>>> filters
	[{'Values': ['test-schedule'], 'Name': 'tag:Availability'}, {'Values': [''], 'Name': 'tag:Backup'}, {'Values': [''], 'Name': 'tag:CostCentre'}, {'Values': [''], 'Name': 'tag:CreatedBy'}, {'Values': [''], 'Name': 'tag:Division'}, {'Values': ['Prod'], 'Name': 'tag:Environment'}, {'Values': [''], 'Name': 'tag:ManagedBy'}, {'Values': ['test-snapshots-2vols'], 'Name': 'tag:Name'}, {'Values': [''], 'Name': 'tag:Owner'}, {'Values': [''], 'Name': 'tag:Tag10'}]
	>>> instances = get_ec2_instances(filters=filters, region='ap-southeast-2', profile='myawsaccount')
	>>> len(instances)
	1
	>>> instances[0]['InstanceId']
	'i-01a9d25e73544287b'

So for 10 tags we get the instance in the response list. Now adding one more existing tag (`Tag11`) to the filter:

	>>> args.tags = "Availability:test-schedule Backup: CostCentre: CreatedBy: Division: Environment:Prod ManagedBy: Name:test-snapshots-2vols Owner: Tag10: Tag11:"
	>>> tags = [ { t.split(":")[0] : t.split(":")[1] } for t in args.tags.split(" ") ]
	>>> filters = [ { "Name": "tag:"+t.keys()[0], "Values": [ t.values()[0] ] } for t in tags ]
	>>> instances = get_ec2_instances(filters=filters, region='ap-southeast-2', profile='myawsaccount')
	>>> len(instances)
	0

As you can see, in case there are 11 tags we get an empty list of instances. Now if remove a different tag (`Tag10`) from the filter, keeping the `Tag11` at the same time to get back to 10 tags in total:

	>>> args.tags = "Availability:test-schedule Backup: CostCentre: CreatedBy: Division: Environment:Prod ManagedBy: Name:test-snapshots-2vols Owner: Tag11:"
	>>> tags = [ { t.split(":")[0] : t.split(":")[1] } for t in args.tags.split(" ") ]
	>>> filters = [ { "Name": "tag:"+t.keys()[0], "Values": [ t.values()[0] ] } for t in tags ]
	>>> instances = get_ec2_instances(filters=filters, region='ap-southeast-2', profile='myawsaccount')
	>>> len(instances)
	1
	>>> instances[0]['InstanceId']
	'i-01a9d25e73544287b'

Below is the reference to the `get_ec2_instances` and the dependent functions that are used in the example:

	@aws_list_result
	@call_aws_api
	def get_ec2_instances(filters=None, region=None, role_arn=None, profile=None):
	    """
	    Wrapper function to get a list of instances, optionally filtering them
	    """
	    ec2_conn = get_aws_client('ec2', region, role_arn, profile)
	    res = ec2_conn.describe_instances(Filters=filters)
	    instances = [i for r in res['Reservations'] for i in r['Instances'] ]
	    return instances

	def get_aws_client(name=None, region=None, role_arn=None, profile=None):
	    """ Obtain an AWS client connection: direct or by assuming a role
	    """
	    session=get_aws_access(region, role_arn, profile)
	    return session.client(name)

	def get_aws_access(region=None, role_arn=None, profile=None):
	    """ Obtain an Autoscale connection: direct or by assuming a role
	    """
	    aws_access = {}
	    if region is None:
	        # FIXME: still need to use boto.utils as this functionality
	        # is lacking in Boto 3, see https://github.com/boto/boto3/issues/313
	        instance_metadata = boto.utils.get_instance_identity()
	        region = instance_metadata['document']['region']
	    if role_arn is None:
	        aws_access['key']=None
	        aws_access['secret']=None
	        aws_access['token']=None
	    else:
	        sts_client = boto3.client('sts')
	        role_obj = sts_client.assume_role(
	            RoleArn=role_arn,
	            RoleSessionName="AssumeRoleSession"
	            )
	        aws_access['key']=role_obj.credentials['AccessKeyId']
	        aws_access['secret']=role_obj.credentials['SecretAccessKey']
	        aws_access['token']=role_obj.credentials['SessionToken']
	    aws_access['profile'] = profile
	    aws_access['region']=region
	    session = boto3.session.Session(region_name=aws_access['region'],
	            aws_access_key_id=aws_access['key'],
	            aws_secret_access_key=aws_access['secret'],
	            aws_session_token=aws_access['token'],
	            profile_name=aws_access['profile'])
	    return session

	class dotdict(dict):
	    """dot.notation access to dictionary attributes
	    >>> dotdict({ "key1": "value1", "key2": "value2" }).key1
	    'value1'
	    >>> dotdict({ "key1": { "key2": "value2"} }).key1
	    {'key2': 'value2'}
	    >>> dotdict({ "key1": { "key2": "value2"} }).key1.key2
	    Traceback (most recent call last):
	      File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
	    AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'key2'
	    """
	    def __getattr__(self, attr):
	        return self.get(attr)
	    __setattr__= dict.__setitem__
	    __delattr__= dict.__delitem__

boto3/boto3/dynamodb/conditions.py

issue_title: setup.cfg references 'pylonshq.com', which is (often, possibly permanently) down
issue_body:
installs apparently break now ( https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pylons-discuss/cypPARmbh_0 )

    [easy_install]
    find_links = http://www.pylonshq.com/download/

According to 'whois', pylonshq belongs to James Gardner.  

Possible fixes:
• Get James to put the site up again
* update the /download/ to serve off pylonsproject.com 
* if this line is removed, easy_install should just hit PyPi

Sorry I'm new to this, but when using a unix socket instead of ip connection with nginx, do I use `proxy_pass` still, or one of the other options: `fastcgi_pass, uwsgi_pass, scgi_pass, memcached_pass`?

Using `scout-apm` with the Core Agent `trace_reporter` branch, I'm seeing a number of traces w/o `Controller` spans, which is unexpected. 

An example: https://gist.github.com/itsderek23/207d51caaa4cc98841246ffae015f2ec
Does this lib apply to python 3.6?

issue_title: "cluster" namespace is very confusing for vcd-cli
issue_body:
Hi @pacogomez,

I think one of the most important issue to fix early on is to change the `vcd cluster` namespace, and probably use `cse` or `kubernetes`(I think `cse` would be better as it is the project name)

a vCloud Director Cluster, is often referred as the vCloud Instance where a collection of cells for a cluster, designed for scale and availability, and has nothing to do with kubernetes nor the container-service-extension (cse) project.

Also some people might maybe confuse it with the vSphere Cluster constructs.

To avoid any confusion, I truly believe it's the first change that would make the most sense.

---

For example
```
tsugliani at retina15 in ~
❯ vcd cluster                                                                       (621ms) 17:13:15
Usage: vcd cluster [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Work with kubernetes clusters in vCloud Director.

      Examples
          vcd cluster list
              Get list of kubernetes clusters in current virtual datacenter.

          vcd cluster create k8s-cluster --nodes 2
              Create a kubernetes cluster in current virtual datacenter.

          vcd cluster delete k8s-cluster
              Deletes a kubernetes cluster by id.


Options:
  -h, --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  config  get cluster config
  create  create cluster
  delete  delete cluster
  list    list clusters
```

Using `cse` namespace

```
tsugliani at retina15 in ~
❯ vcd cse                                                                       (621ms) 17:13:15
Usage: vcd cse [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Work with kubernetes clusters in vCloud Director.

      Examples
          vcd cse list
              Get list of kubernetes clusters in current virtual datacenter.

          vcd cse create k8s-cluster --nodes 2
              Create a kubernetes cluster in current virtual datacenter.

          vcd cse delete k8s-cluster
              Deletes a kubernetes cluster by id.


Options:
  -h, --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  config  get cse kubernetes cluster config 
  create  create cse kubernetes cluster
  delete  delete cse kubernetes cluster
  list    list cse kubernetes cluster
```

Also on the vcloud extensibility side, the API namespaces should be adapted in the same way:

```
vcd system extension create cse cse cse vcdext '/api/cluster, /api/cluster/.*, /api/cluster/.*/.*'
```

Using `cse` namespace

```
vcd system extension create cse cse cse vcdext '/api/cse, /api/cse/.*, /api/cse/.*/.*'
```

or if `cluster` is wanted for clarity

```
vcd system extension create cse cse cse vcdext '/api/cse/cluster, /api/cse/cluster/.*, /api/cse/cluster/.*/.*'
```

Hope this makes sense.

    scan_module(item)

issue_title: Use of GIT_REPO env-var for setting Repo path in 2.0.9
issue_body:
[This change](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/commit/26bb778b34b93537cfbfd5c556d3810f2cf3f76e) in `2.0.9` is a bit problematic:

- Even if a `path` is explicitly provided to the constructor, the `GIT_DIR` environment variable is used instead

- For things like git hooks, `GIT_DIR` is set to `.git` by default. This will result in [`self.git_dir`](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/master/git/repo/base.py#L156) being set to `.git` (`os.path.abspath` instead of `os.path.normpath` would fix this). Consequently, subsequent commands fails (eg: `repo.git.status()`)

  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/pyannotate_tools/annotations/infer.py", line 8, in <module>

  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/pyannotate_tools/annotations/parse.py", line 12, in <module>
    from typing import Any, List, Mapping, Set, Text, Tuple
ImportError: cannot import name 'Text'
```

Can this be fixed? Maybe "hide" the typing import behind an `if True`?

This works as a workaround: 
`PYTHONPATH=/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/ pyannotate`

Currently collecting types has a significant performance impact, and even rewriting type collection in C/Cython would only reduce it so much. It would be nice if the performance impact would be controllable in a way that the lower end would be, say, 1-2%. This would make it practical to collect types on production servers. 

The motivation is that types collected during tests or manually running a program are unlikely to be complete, and during tests it's possible to have mocks and fakes that generate noise. By running in production on a large number of servers, it may be possible to easily collect a fairly complete picture of concrete runtime types, at least for more commonly used functions.

A potential approach is to run the type collector for roughly every N call events. At least the sampling logic would have to be implemented in C (or maybe Cython?) for acceptable performance. If N is large enough, the overhead would be dominated by the cost of invoking the profiling hook + a few machine instructions to decrement a counter and check the value. 

It should be easy to validate the performance impact of the approach. The cost of collecting types isn't very important since we can make N large. However, at some point the collected types will be too sparse to be useful.

This issue doesn't cover how we'd aggregate types collected in multiple processes.

(The proposed approach is not my invention.)

issue_title: Return value of `Register keyword to run on failure` cannot always be used to restore original state
issue_body:
There seems to be an issue with the implementation of the "Register keyword to run on failure" keyword. The keyword returns the value of the previously registered keyword which works fine if this is set to a keyword e.g. "Capture Page Screenshot" but if the registered keyword is set to "None" the return value is the string "No keyword". If you then try to use the return value "No keyword" and pass this back into "Register keyword to run on failure" a warning is generated: 

"Keyword 'No keyword' could not be run on failure: No keyword with name 'No keyword' found."

I guess this keyword should either be returning "Nothing" as a string rather than "No keyword" to be consistent with the current way of disabling the run-on-failure functionality or it could return None instead

Example test case (simplistic example):

```
*** Settings ***
Library           Selenium2Library

*** Test Cases ***
Test Case 1
    ${previous_kw}=    Register Keyword To Run On Failure    Nothing
    ${previous_kw}=    Register Keyword To Run On Failure    Capture Page Screenshot
    Register Keyword To Run On Failure    ${previous_kw}
    Open Browser    http://www.google.com
    Page Should Contain    invalid_string
```

The implementation could be changed to the following which would return None from line 2 of the above test case and no warnings are observed on failure:

```
def register_keyword_to_run_on_failure(self, keyword):
    """Documentation here....
    """
    old_keyword = self._run_on_failure_keyword
    old_keyword_text = old_keyword if old_keyword is not None else "No keyword"

    if keyword:
        new_keyword = keyword if keyword.strip().lower() != "nothing" else None
    else:
        new_keyword = None
    new_keyword_text = new_keyword if new_keyword is not None else "No     keyword"

    self._run_on_failure_keyword = new_keyword
    self._info('%s will be run on failure.' % new_keyword_text)

    return old_keyword
```

issue_title: Auditwheel 1.6 breaks numpy / matplotlib wheel builds
issue_body:
Auditwheel 1.6 seems to be failing to detect and graft the dependencies of the wheels.

For new failure example see:  

https://s3.amazonaws.com/archive.travis-ci.org/jobs/227872764/log.txt?X-Amz-Expires=29&X-Amz-Date=20170502T103338Z&X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&X-Amz-Credential=AKIAJRYRXRSVGNKPKO5A/20170502/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request&X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&X-Amz-Signature=5d2475de4191276eaca340c392322baa82107cf2b2b7848ef553abcf0d5a2130

For failure fixed by downgrading auditwheel to 1.5:

https://s3.amazonaws.com/archive.travis-ci.org/jobs/227948047/log.txt?X-Amz-Expires=30&X-Amz-Date=20170502T141452Z&X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&X-Amz-Credential=AKIAJRYRXRSVGNKPKO5A/20170502/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request&X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&X-Amz-Signature=e902b4ad049fea3e570d803e1a767b35c6e83aedd096e2ebc71206d67384cdab

With the downgrade, I see this in the log (second link, just above):

```
Grafting: /usr/local/lib/libopenblasp-r0.2.18.so -> numpy/.libs/libopenblasp-r0-39a31c03.2.18.so
Grafting: /usr/lib64/libgfortran.so.3.0.0 -> numpy/.libs/libgfortran-ed201abd.so.3.0.0
Setting RPATH: numpy/core/multiarray.so to "$ORIGIN/../.libs"
Setting RPATH: numpy/linalg/_umath_linalg.so to "$ORIGIN/../.libs"
Setting RPATH: numpy/linalg/lapack_lite.so to "$ORIGIN/../.libs"
```

These are missing in the run with new auditwheel 1.6 (first link above).

Seems to be the same problem for matplotlib:

https://s3.amazonaws.com/archive.travis-ci.org/jobs/227868822/log.txt?X-Amz-Expires=30&X-Amz-Date=20170502T145228Z&X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&X-Amz-Credential=AKIAJRYRXRSVGNKPKO5A/20170502/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request&X-Amz-SignedHeaders=host&X-Amz-Signature=3ed4feede56c695aae69cffae255e0392dfb8c4b74d159933df20076c2a06b84

Here it looks like the libpng library was not grafted into the matplotlib wheel.

issue_title: Tests install project under test as dependency
issue_body:
When running the `test` command on a project that has circular dependencies such that the project under test is also depended on by one of the project's dependencies, setuptools will install the depended project onto sys.path, causing problems with import mismatches or tests that run not against the current project, but against a released version.

This issue was reported in jaraco/jaraco.collections#1 as a circular dependency exists between `jaraco.text` <-> `jaraco.collections`.

The issue can be worked around by first running egg_info on the project under test, which because it's in the current working directory allows it to be resolved as the dependency for the dependencies.

It's possible this issue only affects namespace packages and that the [sys.path.insert(0, ...)](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/e726680/setuptools/command/test.py#L145) is sufficient to give simple packages precedence.

When I try running:

`pip install virtualenvwrapper`

I get the following error: 

```
Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /private/tmp/pip-build-np7LOX/virtualenvwrapper/
```

I'm running the command on a mac.

I'm using Win10 and PyCharm2016.2 .
I have python 3.4 which isn't registered in PATH.

Once I have created venv using PyCharm and succesful installed some libraries (using PyCharm again). But then I open cmd, succesful activate venv and type ˋpythonˋ, but cmd return to me error: ˋ"python" command not foundˋ. Then I have checked same command with deactivated venv and get the same error. 

I know how to make python avialable to be called from cmd in my system, but how to do this in virtual environment?

issue_title: linger_ms setting taking precedence over min_queued_messages and sync=True while producing messages
issue_body:
It takes 5sec to send the Kafka message irrespective of the min_queued_messages=1 and sync=True settings. The producer documentation says otherwise http://pykafka.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/producer.html

```Python
 #!/usr/bin/env python

from pykafka import KafkaClient
import json
from datetime import datetime
import time

created_topic     =  'test'
group_id = 'test'
kafka_broker_list = 'localhost:9092,localhost1:9092,localhost2:9092'

payload = {'command': 'test_command', 'task-id':'3', 'key':'123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426655440000'}
payload = {'message':payload, 'creation-time':str(datetime.now())}

client = KafkaClient(hosts=kafka_broker_list)
topic = client.topics[created_topic]

with topic.get_sync_producer() as producer:
        producer.produce(json.dumps(payload))

print payload
```
Below code works fine as expected, sending the message immediately without lingering.
```Python
with topic.get_sync_producer(linger_ms=0) as producer:
        producer.produce(json.dumps(payload))
```
**PyKafka version**: 2.5.0
**Kafka version**: 0.9.0.1

issue_title: Investigate alternative methods for communicating with Storm
issue_body:
Because all communication with Storm currently happens over stdin and stdout, which cannot reliably be read/written in a non-blocking fashion in Python, we should come up with ideas for how else we might communicate with Storm. The simplest method would be to use sockets.

We would need a custom subclass of `ShellProcess` so that we could override [`ShellProcess.launch`](https://github.com/apache/storm/blob/1c2286bc2c776fe14869111070131f1407d698fe/storm-client/src/jvm/org/apache/storm/utils/ShellProcess.java#L71-L99) to pass socket-based input and output streams to the serializer class. We would then need to subclass `ShellBolt` so that we could modify [this line](https://github.com/apache/storm/blob/1c2286bc2c776fe14869111070131f1407d698fe/storm-client/src/jvm/org/apache/storm/task/ShellBolt.java#L148) in `ShellBolt.prepare` to use the new subclass.

There are probably other approaches we could take too, so let's hash them out here as they come up.

issue_title: DiffScreen.draw() marks wrong line as dirty when wrapping occurs
issue_body:
The current implementation of `DiffScreen.draw()` in `pyte/screens.py` as at 12276a8 is that it first marks the row containing the cursor as dirty and then calls `Screen.draw()`.

However, the latter method will, if wrapping is enabled (`if mo.DECAWM in self.mode`), move to the next line before actually writing the character to the virtual terminal.  This means that the wrong line might have been marked as dirty.

It seems to me that simply switching the order of the statements in `DiffScreen.draw()` would fix this issue.

issue_title: Have a standardized way of testing correctness
issue_body:
For testing correctness, the pattern is always going to be the same - create a region with some arguments, then check that calling certain fixed methods or properties return a given result. We could therefore simplify the boilerplate by simply making it possible to have a list of regions to test and provide reference results. One way to do this would be to have the regions stored in a serialized way, and have a mapping from serialized file to reference results. For instance, we could have a table with the following format (just an idea to start the discussion):

```
region_file,    area,    to_mask,         to_pixel
region1.xml,     5.4,  mask1.fits, region1_pix.xml
region2.xml,     2.3,  mask2.fits, region2_pix.xml
```

This would save writing a lot of code and make it easier to have many tests for corner cases, etc. It would also be easy to inspect things like masks.

Ideally we would use a serialization format simpler than XML for this so that these can be written by hand, not generated.

issue_title: Spatial filter seems to match positions outside region
issue_body:
The spatial filter seems to match positions which are not part of the specified region.  In the following (very slow) test script, I test points over the whole sky using a 1 degree grid.  The output has, as well as the two expected circles, lines at RA = +/- 90 degrees and a few other scattered points.  This was with Astropy 1.0.6 and the current master version of pyregion from this repository.

```python
from __future__ import print_function                                           
from astropy.units import degree                                                
from astropy.wcs import WCS                                                     
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt                                                 
import pyregion                                                                 
                                                                                
region = pyregion.parse('''                                                     
    fk5                                                                         
    circle(0:00:00.000,00:00:00.00,36000")                                      
    circle(3:00:00.000,75:00:00.00,36000")                                      
''')                                                                            
                                                                                
ras = []                                                                        
decs = []                                                                       
                                                                                
for ra in range(-179, 180):                                                     
    for dec in range (-89, 90):                                                 
        wcs = WCS(naxis=2)                                                      
        wcs.wcs.radesys = 'ICRS'                                                
        wcs.wcs.ctype = ['RA---TAN', 'DEC--TAN']                                
        wcs.wcs.cunit = [degree, degree]                                        
        wcs.wcs.crpix = [1, 1]                                                  
        wcs.wcs.cdelt = [-0.0003, 0.0003]                                       
        wcs.wcs.crval = [ra, dec]                                               
                                                                                
        if region.get_filter(header=wcs.to_header()).inside1(1, 1):             
            #print(ra, dec)                                                     
            ras.append(ra)                                                      
            decs.append(dec)                                                    
                                                                                
plt.scatter(ras, decs)                                                          
plt.xlim(-180, 180)                                                             
plt.ylim(-90, 90)                                                               
plt.show() 
```

issue_title: Crash in adjust_compiler when usable compiler isn't found
issue_body:
This showed up via a Stack Overflow question: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/32354982/problems-installing-astropy

If the `get_compiler_version` function fails to find the right way to get the compiler version, it returns the string `'unknown'`.  But the regexp in `adjust_compiler` that checks the compiler version is a bytes regexp.  On Python 2 this is no problem, but on Python 3 it crashes if passed the string `'unknown'`.

This can be replicated by setting the CC environment variable to any program that just exits with a non-zero error code (regardless of arguments).

Note, even if not for this bug the installation would probably still fail if the user lacks a working compiler.

It would be good if you could document the new `auto_paging_iter` method for listable object in the Pagination docs: https://stripe.com/docs/api/python#pagination

```customers = stripe.Customer.auto_paging_iter()

for customer in customers:
    do_something(customer)
```

From the documentation at https://stripe.com/docs/api/python#auto_pagination:

> To use the auto-pagination feature in Python, simply issue an initial "list" call with the parameters you need, then call auto_paging_iter() on the returned list object to iterate over all objects matching your initial parameters.

customers = stripe.Customer.all(limit=3)

issue_body:
Consider the following snippet from the `CollectionWrapper` implementation in `engine/__init__.py`:

```python
  def keys(self):
    keys = set()
    for i in range(self.collection.getCount()):
      keys.add(self.collection.getByIndex(i)[0])
    return keys

  def values(self):
    values = set()

    for i in range(self.collection.getCount()):
      p = self.collection.getByIndex(i)
      values.add(self.valueWrapper(p[0], p[1]))
    return values

  def items(self):
    items = set()
    for i in range(self.collection.getCount()):
      p = self.collection.getByIndex(i)
      items.add((p[0], self.valueWrapper(p[0], p[1])))
    return items
```

Now, consider this snippet from the [Python documentation re: dict objects](https://docs.python.org/2/library/stdtypes.html#dict.items)...

> If items(), keys(), values(), iteritems(), iterkeys(), and itervalues() are called with no intervening modifications to the dictionary, the lists will directly correspond. This allows the creation of (value, key) pairs using zip(): pairs = zip(d.values(), d.keys()). The same relationship holds for the iterkeys() and itervalues() methods: pairs = zip(d.itervalues(), d.iterkeys()) provides the same value for pairs. Another way to create the same list is pairs = [(v, k) for (k, v) in d.iteritems()].

In Python, sets are unordered and because of their use in `CollectionWrapper.keys()` and `CollectionWrapper.values()`, and `CollectionWrapper.items()`, there's no guarantee of consistent ordering between the two, potentially resulting in a violation of the assumptions that `zip(d.keys(), d.values()) == d.items()` as is consistent with `dict` and `dict`-like objects in Python.

`CollectionWrapper.keys()` and `CollectionWrapper.values()` should iterate in the same order, producing lists (not sets).  `CollectionWrapper.items()` could then be simplified to something like:

```python
def items(self):
  return zip(self.keys(), self.values())
```

issue_title: BibUpload: update fails if original record is broken
issue_body:
Originally on 2010-08-02

When a record of the database needs to be fixed, but the inserted version of the record cannot be parsed by BibRecord (typically because it contains invalid XML chars, possibly inserted by a previous version of BibUpload that did not check for XML-valid input, or inserted through some unconventional/deprecated means), it is not possible to replace this erroneous record by a valid MARCXML with BibUpload (in --replace mode)

The replacement is made impossible by the fact that BibUpload tries to load the previous record to keep the fields in `CFG_BIBUPLOAD_STRONG_TAGS`.

It might be interesting to have a special replacement mode that skips the original record (and hence CFG_BIBUPLOAD_STRONG_TAGS). Although this might be useful only to a very limited number of repositories running Invenio, it might still be good to have it in case BibUpload/BibRecord strengthens its reqirements in the future, and existing records would need to be "migrated".

Originally on 2010-10-11

Tabs ("References", "Discussion", etc.) on detailed record pages miss on purpose the 'ln=XX' parameter when the page is viewed in language 'CFG_SITE_LANG' (for eg: http://invenio-demo.cern.ch/record/96?ln=en): the consequence is that the browser preferred language is used when clicking on these tabs even if the user has specifically chosen to view the detailed page in language 'CFG_SITE_LANG'.

issue_title: Abstract for match in regex: pattern in s_e_q_p
issue_body:
Originally on 2010-07-09

search_engine_query_parser uses this coding pattern many times:

```python
results = ''
position = 0
for match in regexp(query):
  results += query[position:match.start()]
  # do stuff with match
  position = match.end()
results += query[position:]
return results
```

Since this is done n >= 3 times, this pattern should be captured into a function which accepts a regexp to match with, a function to apply and a string to do it with.  

This will make seqp easier to read and very probably kill as-yet-unsuspected bugs.

global: load tasks via entry points
issue_body:
Add support for loading tasks via entry_points and modules.

Invenio modules should be using the [``@shared_task``](http://docs.celeryproject.org/en/latest/django/first-steps-with-django.html#using-the-shared-task-decorator) decorator to declare tasks.

The [celery application](https://github.com/lnielsen/mysite/blob/master/mysite/celery.py) needs a way to load these tasks. This should be possible via providing entry points and/or list of modules.

```python
# setup.py (declaring celery tasks in a Invenio module).
setup(
    entry_points={
        'invenio_celery.tasks': [
            'invenio_fungenerator = invenio_fungenerator',
        ]
    }
)
```

The celery application should then be able to load the tasks similar to this:
```python
from flask_celeryext import create_celery_app
from invenio_celery import load_tasks
from .factory import create_app
celery = create_celery_app(create_app)
celery.autodiscover_tasks(load_tasks(entry_point='invenio_celery.tasks'))
```
Update documentation structure as decided in https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/issues/56.

issue_body:
Hello,

```python

try:
    PersistentIdentifier.create('doi', '10.1234/foo')
except PIDAlreadyExists:
    pass
```

I still got an exception:
`sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError: (psycopg2.IntegrityError) duplicate key value violates unique constraint "uidx_type_pid"`

If I understand well this exception raised at the end of the scope session. Do you have to do a `rollback` or is it done automatically at the end of the with session.begin_nested()?

Python: 3.5.2, SqlAlchemy with Postgresql.

The package contains `examples/app.py` example application, but there is no test
for it in `tests/test_example_app.py`.  It should be added.  See existing examples:

- https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records/blob/master/tests/test_example_app.py
- https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/blob/master/tests/test_example_app.py

TOC:
- Features
- Overview
   - Understanding API Authentication in Invenio
     - Session (Secure HTTP only cookie) + CSRF token (JWT) usually a browser
        - ``Cookie: ...`` + ``Authorization: Bearer <CSRF token>``
     - Access token usually a machine (TODO: what about implicit grant - would need a CSRF token as well).
        - ``Authorization: Bearer <token>`` or ``?access_token=<token>``
   - Obtaining a session and CSRF token
      - Login (Invenio-Accounts) to obtain a session 
      - CSRF token - use Jinja context processor ``{{ jwt() }}`` or ``{{jwt_token()}}``) to embed CSRF token in DOM tree.  (this should probably be added by default to Search-UI)
   - Obtaining an access token
       - Personal access token (API key)
       - OAuth 2.0 Flows: (see nice [doc](https://auth0.com/docs/api-auth/which-oauth-flow-to-use) from Auth0)
          - Actors: Client, Resource Owner, Resource Server
          - Client credentials grant (client is also resource owner)
          - Authorization code grant (client is a web app on a server or a native application)
          - Implicit grant (Single Page Application - JavaScript)
          - Password grant (disabled by default)
       - Authorization Code Grant
       - Client credentials grant
       - Implicit grant:
          - TODO: check life time of tokens, and confidential vs public clients. Understand security implications for SPAs (e.g implicit tokens must not be refreshable).
       - Refreshing an access token   
   - Security considerations: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) considerations
- Usage:
   - Protecting your REST API with authentication
     - Register blueprints on API application.  By default, the API application has a [``before_request``](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-oauth2server/blob/master/invenio_oauth2server/ext.py#L181) that will check an access token if is used and ``current_user`` 
     - If you want to require authentication use ``@require_api_auth()`` (similar to ``@login_required`` in the UI). 
   - Delegating rights via scopes
     - ``@require_oauth_scopes()``
   - Registering a client
  



@lnielsen commented on [Mon Aug 18 2014](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/issues/2115)
- [ ] OAuth 2.0 Overview 
- [ ] Normal flow
- [ ] JavaScript flow
- [ ] Native app flow
- [x] Endpoint documentation
- [ ] Integrate with REST API docuemntation

As we allow more and more import strings in modules configuration, moving `obj_or_import_string` in `invenio-utils` might be useful.

If time is a concern we can deprecate the one in `invenio-records-rest` and create a new one in `invenio-utils`

issue_title: global: accept pure dict RECORDS_REST_FACETS config variable
issue_body:
Look at the example for such config variable https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/master/examples/app.py#L174  .
The `filters` and `post_filters` fields are functions and the https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/master/invenio_records_rest/facets.py expects a function to execute.
In order to use systems as Rancher where you can create a different config for each instance, we should be able to provide a pure dict config variable. 
So I propose to accept a dictionary (for example {'terms':{'field':'fieldname'}} ) in the filters and post_filters fields.

Django Model Backend's authenticate method checks as follows, 
 

    if user.check_password(password) and self.user_can_authenticate(user):

And user_can_authenticate method is 

     def user_can_authenticate(self, user):
            """

            that attribute are allowed.
            """
           is_active = getattr(user, 'is_active', None)

      if user: <- This is None, for inactive user.

As of Python 3.3, `IOError` is being merged into `OSError`. issue_title: max_drawdown incorrect if drawdown begins on first day of returns time series
issue_body:
Because we take cum_returns(returns, starting_value=1) to form the NAV time series off of which we calculate max drawdown, the first value of the NAV used in the max drawdown calculation is the portfolio value at the end of the first trading day. This means that when the first trading day is the first day of the max drawdown, we are not counting that day's loss in that max drawdown stat.

For example:

If the first trading day results in a 10% loss and subsequent trading days lead to a total 25% loss, we will be calculating max drawdown as:
(.90 - 0.75) / 0.90  = 0.16666666

when we should be calculating it as:
(1.0 - 0.75) / 1.0 =  0.25

I think the fix might be to append a dummy day with a value of 1 to the beginning of the `df_cum` time series in cum_returns in the case where there is no NaN in the first iloc.

issue_title: factor_returns should shift returns by one day
issue_body: The format in alphalens is: signal_t <> return_(t+T). In words, the signal on day t is linked to the returns of that asset on day t+T (e.g. 1). `factor_returns` computes the return series of that signal by multiplying `signal_t * return_(t+T)` and giving that timestamp `t`. However, I'm arguing it should be timestamp `t+T` because only then is the return actually realized.

issue_title: Bad SPARQLStore handling of empty literals
issue_body:
When an empty literal is returned to a SPARQLStore, it becomes an `rdflib.term.Literal(u'None')` instead of an `rdflib.term.Literal(u'')`:

```python
# ./fuseki-server --update --mem /empty_literal

import rdflib
from rdflib.plugins.stores.sparqlstore import SPARQLUpdateStore

store = SPARQLUpdateStore('http://fuseki.til.io:3030/empty_literal/query',
                          'http://fuseki.til.io:3030/empty_literal/update')

graph = rdflib.Graph(store, 'http://example.com/')

graph.add((rdflib.URIRef('http://example.com/s'), 
           rdflib.URIRef('http://example.com/p'), 
           rdflib.Literal('')))

o = tuple(graph)[0][2]
assert o == rdflib.Literal(''), repr(o)
```
raises an `AssertionError: rdflib.term.Literal(u'None')`



The problem seems to be in `rdflib.plugins.stores.sparqlstore:CastToTerm`:

```python
import io
from xml.etree import ElementTree

import rdflib
from rdflib.plugins.stores.sparqlstore import CastToTerm

s = """<ns0:literal xmlns:ns0="http://www.w3.org/2005/sparql-results#" />"""
node = ElementTree.parse(io.BytesIO(s)).getroot()
term = CastToTerm(node)
assert term  == rdflib.Literal(''), repr(term)
```
raises an `AssertionError: rdflib.term.Literal(u'None')`

issue_title: SPARQL result in XML may not be strictly valid?
issue_body:
The XML serializer for SPARQL results produces a result that looks valid to me, but is not recognized by at least one tool (namely [Corese](http://wimmics.inria.fr/corese)). The problem comes from the fact that the serializer produces something like

```xml
  <sparql:sparql xmlns:sparql="http://www.w3.org/2005/sparql-results#">
```
while Corese expects an empty NS prefix, as in

```xml
  <sparql xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/sparql-results#">
```
which is actually [what the recommendation says](http://www.w3.org/TR/rdf-sparql-XMLres/#docElement). Again, the fact that the documentation uses XML namespaces makes me think that what RDFLib produces is valid, but the phrasing ("written as follow") makes it ambiguous. I explicitly [asked the question on the public mailing list](https://lists.w3.org/Archives/Public/public-sparql-dev/2015JulSep/0007.html).

Whatever the answer is, I guess it wouldn't hurt to be a little more conservative in RDFLib. If I'm correct, changing [that line](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/blob/7f6c9e73c2b7b84fd38811b3064e3740f96d7976/rdflib/plugins/sparql/results/xmlresults.py#L148) to use the empty prefix rather than the "sparql" prefix would do the trick (sorry, not really able to test it myself right now).

issue_title: Add ability to overwrite existing NFF datasets
issue_body:
* Add flag `overwrite` (by default `False`) to the `dt.save(dest)` call.
* If path `dest` exists (and is not an empty folder) and `overwrite` is False, then an exception should be thrown. However if `overwrite` is True, then the content of this folder should be replaced with the datatable being saved.
* Special care should be taken to account for the case when the datatable being saved is a modified version of the datatable that's already in that folder. (Or more generally -- what would happen if some of the files in that folder are memory-mapped in another datatable? what if they are not?)

Hello all!

I have some suggestions for new Accessibility Icons.

ColorADD has come out with universal color symbols to aid those suffering from color-blindness, but these symbols may be used everywhere to help differentiate colors. These could be added onto legends next to pie/bar charts to help those understand the colors. The card game UNO has just started to incorporate the symbols.

More information can be found here:
http://www.coloradd.net/code.asp

The current fa-square and fa-square-o could be used, or similar and larger ones may need to be added, to allow for the stacking of smaller icons inside.
While stacking is almost the idea behind ColorADD, they couldn't be stacked easily in FA. For instance, "purple" and "brown" are more different than just a line between the two wedges.

If we started with:
"fa-coloradd-blue, fa-coloradd-green, fa-coloradd-yellow, fa-coloradd-orange, fa-coloradd-red, fa-coloradd-purple, fa-coloradd-brown"
we would have the seven basic colors.
From there, stacking would work to create the seven color-light and seven color-dark icons. (Lighter colors could be shown stacked with a *new* 'fa-coloradd-white' and darker colors could be shown with 'fa-inverse' and a *new* 'fa-coloradd-black'.

That just leaves creating four more. Two of them (fa-coloradd-grey-light and fa-coloradd-grey-dark) could be made from one smaller-squircle (fa-coloradd-grey?) and just fa-inverse'd and stacked as needed.
The last two gold and silver require creating, in their terms, a parenthesis to the left of a large squircle; one coloradd-white and one coloradd-black. Gold and silver would likely require them being their own call out "fa-coloradd-gold" and "fa-coloradd-silver" because of alignments if we tried to stack icons.

And finally, if this is a duplicate, I am sorry. The Git search and Advanced Search appears to not be all that good at searching for previous suggestions. :(

issue_title: tox: Should the py26 testenv be kept ?
issue_body:
As a potential contributor, the first thing I do when starting to work on a project is run the test suite.

When I clone `tower-cli` and run `tox` I end up with:

```
docs: commands succeeded                                                  
flake8: commands succeeded                                                
ERROR:   py26: commands failed                                            
py27: commands succeeded                                                  
py34: commands succeeded                                                  
py35: commands succeeded                                                  
py36: commands succeeded   
```

They are 208 occurrences of the same failure: `ValueError: zero length field name in format`.
(ie. `prefix = urljoin(prefix, "{}/".format(CUR_API_VERSION))`)

It happens because the format expression has been used without indices. Expression that has become valid in py27+ and py32+.

So should the py26 tox testenv be kept?
We should see about reading TOWER_HOST, TOWER_USERNAME, etc. from environment variables and using those instead of looking to the config file.

issue_title: Provide recipe to update YAML files (parameters and tests)
issue_body:
Hi there!

I really enjoy OpenFisca, but I recently encountered an issue.

### Here is what I did:
I work on OpenFisca-France and I tried to move ASI from a _family_ variable to an _individu_ variables. 

### Here is what I expected to happen:
There are hundreds of tests in OpenFisca-France. And a lot of them have ASI as a family variable.
I wanted to migrate those test files with a script.

I created the following snippet to check if I could read/write a YAML file without changing its structure (ie. without reordering its keys).

(The following script can be ran from the root of the openfisca-france repository).
```
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

import yaml
import pprint

filename = 'test_mes_aides_54d4e0704ec19fce442273a0.yaml'

with open(filename, 'r') as f:
	data = yaml.safe_load(f)

with open(filename, 'w') as f:
	yaml.safe_dump(data, f, default_flow_style=False, allow_unicode=True)
```

### Here is what actually happened:
Keys are reordered and the file is therefore modified.

Would you have a recipe to read/write those files without much impact?
(I will investigate in NodeJS)

## Context

I identify more as a:

- Developer _(I create tools that use the existing OpenFisca code)_.

Following a question from Robert Cauneau, we've noticed that some input variable can't be divided into months.

cf : https://jupyter.openfisca.fr/notebooks/users/robert/Etudes%20de%20variables/Test_Retraite_divided.ipynb.

For example: the `retraite_brute`    
But the problem is that some variables are based upon this one (as `csg_deductible_retraite`) and that these variables are calculated on a monthly basis. So there are every time put to zero.

Looking at the code, I think that only one line is missing in the definition of `retraite_brute` in comparison to other variables` :   
`set_input = set_input_divide_by_period`.

Ths issue concerns at least : `retraite_brute`, `chomage_brut`

But also all monthly variables that you want to add as entry variable in the init_single_entity method.

issue_title: Guideline pour les tests qui ne passent pas
issue_body:
Y-a-t-il des directives pour les tests qui ne passent pas ?
@Benjello semble dire que non ( voir [#386](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/386) )

Ce que l'on pourrait faire est :
* Si la formule ne change pas d'année en année, qu'un certains nombre de tests passent, mais que des cas particuliers ne passent pas, on pourrait commenter ces cas particuliers au sein du yaml, commiter les tests commentés, ouvrir une issue référençant l'ensemble des lignes ne passant pas.

* Si la formule n'est pas testée (ou que c'est une dated_formula non testée pour ses années), créer un fichier nonpassing_test_formulaname.yaml qui serait exclu des tests travis de par son nom ; ouvrir une issue.

issue_title: Incorrect initialisation of resources.Issue causes fields, id and key fields to be reset
issue_body:
In f1866f48ea349d5c92cbdd11893b4b742d1f9a3c a change was made to add 'type hints' to the `Issue` class in the `jira.resources` module. However, due to the ordering of this new code, an `Issue` instance initialised from a `raw` dict would see its `fields`, `id` and `key` properties reset to `None`:

```
def __init__(self, options, session, raw=None):
        Resource.__init__(self, 'issue/{0}', options, session)
        if raw:
            self._parse_raw(raw)

        self.fields = None
        """ :type : Issue._IssueFields """
        self.id = None
        """ :type : int """
        self.key = None
        """ :type : str """
```

This means that, for example, an `Issue` instantiated from search results (`jira.search_issues()`)does not have its `fields` data accessible, whereas one instantiated using `jira.issue()` is fully functional. This side effect is readily noticeable from a broken test - `IssueTests.test_issue_equal()`. I note that this test was marked as failing in dedb4447eea6fe89c2c8fc2d5edf3a478ae8d599, 9 days after the introduction of the issue above.

Moving the `if raw` block after the type hint initialisers fixes the problem, and the test passes.

issue_title: IO redirection in the print_*() methods.
issue_body: For help in debugging large projects (for example I would like to use this for my project at http://www.nmr-relax.com/), it would be of great benefit if the pympler.tracker.SummaryTracker.print_diff(), pympler.summary.print_() and related string formatting methods could be modified to allow for IO redirection.  This will allow the pympler output to be separated from the verbose program output when performing large tests.  A simple 'file' keyword argument would be sufficient which, if set to None, would default to sys.stdout.  Cheers!

 # List of valid names for the first argument in a metaclass class method.
 # DEFAULT:  valid-metaclass-classmethod-first-arg=mcs
 
+# List of member names, which should be excluded from the protected access
+# warning.
+exclude-protected=_asdict,_fields,_replace,_source,_make
+
 
 [DESIGN]
 
@@ -400,6 +436,9 @@ min-public-methods=0
 # RATIONALE:  API mapping
 max-public-methods=40
 
+# Maximum number of boolean expressions in a if statement
+max-bool-expr=5
+
 
 [EXCEPTIONS]
 
```

-----

Some things worth noting
- the default list of disabled went from empty to [include 50 things][1]
- max line length went from 80 to 100 (but AFAIK it's 79 in the `pep8` defaults)
- adding `[MASTER].jobs` to speed up execution (happy, happy)
- a `[SPELLING]` section was enabled for spellcheck (via `python-enchant`)
- `[FORMAT].expected-line-ending-format` added (so we can disallow Windows line endings)

[1]: https://gist.github.com/dhermes/f1586b902d8c67c0a7df

Basically like ```upload_from_string```, but without text detection. And without ```content_type``` set to ```text/plain``` as default. Instead, ```content_type``` should be guessed from the name of the blob.

It's currently not possible to send pre-compressed content without either explicitly setting a ```content_type``` or making an unnecessary temporary file.

And ```download_as_string``` should perhaps be renamed ```download_as_bytes``` then, which is what it does.

issue_title: Should we use an 'enum' type for enumerated values?

issue_body:
I have storage as my publisher and a subscriber on the compute engine as the receiver. 
Below is the subscriber code that I use.
```
def receive_messages_with_flow_control(project, subscription_name):
    """Receives messages from a pull subscription with flow control."""
    subscriber = pubsub_v1.SubscriberClient()
    subscription_path = subscriber.subscription_path(
        project, subscription_name)
    idocStorageAbsolutePath = config.idocsStorageAbsolutePath
    makeDirIfNotExist(idocStorageAbsolutePath)
    def callback(message):
        storage_data = message.data
        process_data(storage_data,idocStorageAbsolutePath)
        message.ack()

    # Limit the subscriber to only have ten outstanding messages at a time.
    flow_control = pubsub_v1.types.FlowControl(max_messages=50)
    subscriber.subscribe(
        subscription_path, callback=callback, flow_control=flow_control)

    # The subscriber is non-blocking, so we must keep the main thread from
    # exiting to allow it to process messages in the background.
    logger.info('Listening for messages on {}'.format(subscription_path))
    while True:
        time.sleep(5)
```
After a day or two of the subscriber running, it stops consuming the messages. I have to manually restart it to make it work again.  
Please suggest what I am doing wrong.

PS: The data flowing speed into the topic is high during the day and reduces at night.

issue_title: missing support for valid datastore value types
issue_body:
There are a number of data types on https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/java/datastore/entities#Java_Properties_and_value_types that I don't see support for when building Value objects through this API: https://cloud.google.com/datastore/docs/apis/javadoc/com/google/api/services/datastore/DatastoreV1.Value.Builder

For example, com.google.appengine.api.users.User, com.google.appengine.api.datastore.PostalAddress, etc.

I have been using SymPy recently. Anonymous functions can be generated using `sympy.lambdify`.
I required to store these for later, as they can be computationally expensive to generate the expressions in the first place; i.e., solving an ODE system symbolically.

`dill` came to mind as I have previously used it to pickle a lambda, however it was giving errors. Here is a minimalistic example of a simple function:

```python
import sympy
import dill

# use basic session, puts multiple symbols in scope
sympy.init_session()

# create expression, simple for example
expr = 2*x + y

# generate anonymous function
func = sympy.lambdify((x, y), expr)

print(func(5, 6))  #=> prints 16

# pickle file
pickled_func = dill.dumps(func)
```
Where I get a big error that traces back to:
> PicklingError: Can't pickle : it's not found as mpmath.ctx_mp_python.constant

Now I thought this maybe an issue with how SymPy generates the function, however `cloudpickle` was able to successfully pickle the function:

```python
import pickle
import cloudpickle

print(pickle.loads(cloudpickle.dumps(func))(5, 6))  #=> prints 16
```

It was brought to my attention that this may be unexpected behaviour with how `dill` interprets lambdas of objects; and thus you should be made aware.
maybe overwriting the standard library isn't a great idea -- so an alternate install could be provided as the default (with option to overload?)

issue_title: jvm library targets should not be invalidated by transitive dependencies

issue_body:
From `distribution.py`:

```
    First looks through the paths listed for this operating system in the --jvm-distributions-paths
    map. Then looks in JDK_HOME and JAVA_HOME if defined, falling back to a search on the PATH.
```

I think this search order is backwards.  `PATH` and `JAVA_HOME` are explicit configurations given by the operator.    All else being equal (ie it still satisfies min/max version requirements) I'd expect pants to use the version of java I told my OS was the default.

supports #6001.

the idea here would be to create a new `Console` class + instance that we can inject into the `Graph` at startup - which (for now) `@rules` can request and then call `Console.stdout.write('some text')` to have it go to the runners stdout.

we could potentially use a `RootRule` for this like so: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/engine/legacy/options_parsing.py#L43

and this could probably be a Python object to start and could just get a reference to the current value of `sys.std{out|err}` at call time to emit logging. #6005 will probably end up converting this into a Rust-backed thing.

issue_title: Add debug flag to ivy Task.
issue_body:
Ivy report does not show excluded artifacts summary. 
At twitter we have automatic excludes turned on.
A transitive round trip source dep was pulled during ivy resolution. Running resolve with --ivy was not helping us identify which jar was pulling this round trip dep. 

In order to get to the bottom of this, i had to run pants n debug mode.
Construct the ivy command and then add -debug flag.

Adding this flag as an option for debug option passing ivy would be great  help.

issue_title: jvm_binary resources are not jarred up
issue_body:
By inspection, jvm_binary targets are skipped in the JarCreate task: The issue is specific to jvm_binary targets with resources ({java,scala}_library should work fine) - see: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/tasks/jar_create.py#L32

The binary task makes an attempt at getting the jvm_binary resources here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/tasks/binary_create.py#L94

BundleCreate however relies on the JarCreate products: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/tasks/bundle_create.py#L120

So sorting of all this across JarCreate, BinaryCreate & BundleCreate is needed.

issue_title: monitor-bleu option does not exist anymore
issue_body:
The WMT15 English-German translation [example](https://github.com/awslabs/sockeye/blob/master/tutorials/wmt/README.md) uses --monitor-bleu arg, which no longer exists in top-of-tree Sockeye.

I'm not sure if the solution is to change the metric from perplexity to BLEU for monitoring, or keep monitoring perplexity, and report BLEU after training, by post-processing the inference output. However, the example won't work in its current state.

To make browsing Sonos favorites possible with the existing music library methods, a data structure for the `object.itemobject.item.sonos_favorite` Didl class is required.

I have created this class, but before I set up a Pull Request require your advice for code style:
In the metadata for a Sonos favorite, there is a `<resMD>` tag which contains the metadata of the item to which the favorite refers. To make its contents accessible to the user, there are two possible implementations:

1. When the DidlFavorite object is created, parse the resMD metadata and make the referenced object available via an attribute which can be accessed and modified. When the favorite it is converted back to XML, call `to_didl_string()` on the referenced object to get the metadata back.
This can be archieved by adding a `resource_meta_data` property - see https://github.com/ghcs27/SoCo/tree/favorite_data_structure.
2. Keep the resMD metadata in its own attribute. Make it accessible by adding a `reference` property which parses the metadata on read. For this, see https://github.com/ghcs27/SoCo/tree/favorite_data_structure_alt.

Which implementation do you prefer?

### Observed behavior

After setting `KOLIBRI_LISTEN_PORT=9090` in `/etc/kolibri/daemon.conf`, then starting via `service kolibri start` the server still tried to start on port 8080 (which is occupied on my system).

### Expected behavior

Kolibri should start on the port defined by `KOLIBRI_LISTEN_PORT` in `/etc/kolibri/daemon.conf`

### User-facing consequences

Can't start the server on an alternate port automatically using services

### Fix

The following will fix the problem:

`sed -i '/export KOLIBRI_LISTEN$/ s/LISTEN$/LISTEN_PORT/' /etc/default/kolibri`

Minor issue with no user-facing impact. Some apps use URLs that look like:

```
/coach/#/6e54aec31b1a5672ec663c5e11b354c3/groups
```

Others use URLs that look like:

```
/user#/profile

Note that there is no `/` between `user` and `#`. This is also the case for management app URLs - both `device` and `facility`.

Recommend we standardize on URLs that have a `/` before the `#`.

issue_title: When using postgres, unable to import content nodes with long field values
issue_body:
<!--
Instructions:
 * Fill out the sections below, replace …'s with information about your issue
 * Use the 'preview' function above this text box to verify formatting before submitting
-->

### Observed behavior
<!--
Description of the behavior that was observed, including screenshots or other references when applicable
-->

When running Kolibri with a Postgres DB backend, trying to import content that contains a `ContentNode` with `description` or `license_description` longer than 400 chars throws an error (see below).

### Expected behavior
<!--
Description of what behavior was expected but did not occur
-->

When we run with a SQLite backend, it [happily ignores](https://chrisbartos.com/articles/sqlite-doesnt-care-about-your-max_length/) the violation of the `max_length` attribute on these fields. Postgres is more strict (as it should be).

Expectation would be one of the following:
- Don't allow published databases to have strings > 400 char in these fields to start with. The ship's probably already sailed on that one...
- Truncate these strings during import, to respect the 400 char limit. Simple, but kind of ugly.
- Increase this limit within the Kolibri schema. This is probably preferred...

### User-facing consequences
<!--
Implications and real-world consequences for learners, coaches, admins, and other users of the application
-->

Prevents use of Postgres backend for many content sources (e.g. KA). We now have users wanting/trying to use Postgres, and we want to do performance testing on the same.

### Errors and logs
<!--
Relevant logs from:
 * the command line

issue_title: Upstream bug: Zero in first position of buffer to write() will be removed (Mac OS X)
issue_body:
Just a heads up to anyone using this library on a Mac, you may hit this bug:

<https://github.com/signal11/hidapi/issues/396>

If you write, for example, 64 zero bytes, only 63 will be actually sent. A leading zero in any write will be removed, and the buffer shortened by one. You can work around by providing an extra leading zero in those cases.

Related: it would be nice to be able to know the version of the underlying `hidapi` at runtime.

issue_title: Interop problem with Apache CXF causes "Found element {<namespace>}<returnMessageName> but could not find matching RPC/Literal part" when using rpc/literal
issue_body:
Basically, Apache CXF expects no namespaces in response message, but currently xml default namespace is set for response message if a global namespace is used.

At https://github.com/pysimplesoap/pysimplesoap/blob/master/pysimplesoap/server.py#L256
the WS main namespace is added to the response message, and at:
https://github.com/pysimplesoap/pysimplesoap/blob/master/pysimplesoap/simplexml.py#L75
"xmlns=<namespace>" is set, but it seems that Apache CXF expects for rpc / literal no namespaces.

issue_title: Figure out how to handle version changes
issue_body:
See https://lists.ubuntu.com/archives/juju/2017-January/008479.html for context.

Loading the correct facade version dynamically is the way to go. The schema json file will need to be appended to instead of overwritten so that old facade versions are preserved. API classes will need to be namespaced so that two versions of the same facade (with the same name) don't collide.

That's the easy part. The tricky part is the coupling between the OO and api layers. For example, Application.add_units() calls ApplicationFacade.AddUnits() with parameters. What happens when the next version of that facade changes the parameters? The OO layer needs some way to deal with that...

issue_title: New worksheet creation silently fails when not signed in
issue_body:
If you're not signed in and you click on "New Worksheet" and then click "Cancel", you can no longer click the "New Worksheet" button until you refresh the page.

Steps to reproduce:
0) Sign out
1) Go to some CodaLab worksheet (e.g., https://worksheets.codalab.org/worksheets/0x20ce2b583c7a4ddd9496b480c0bc1b79/)
2) Click the "New Worksheet" button in the top right
3) Click "Cancel" without typing anything. This creates an uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'user_name' of null at toggleNewWorksheet.
4) Try to click "New Worksheet" again. 

For usability, it might also be helpful to return the "Not authorized" message upon clicking on "New Worksheet" when you're not signed in (or just grey the button out), instead of letting the user type in a worksheet name before saying that it's not authorized in the console.

issue_title: Does a `ReadTimeoutError` mean there are no more `Client.stats(...)` to be collected?
issue_body:

The `StopIteration` errors are to be expected however I am not sure how to handle the `ReadTimeoutError` errors, does this mean that there are no more stats to be collected and that I should treat it as `StopIteration` or that I should try and ignore the error and keep trying to get the `next()` item from the stats stream?

It seems from my intuition and experimentation that  `ReadTimeoutError` is equivalent to `StopIteration` for the case of collection container stats as I seem to get them almost interchangeably.

Could you also tell me how the stats stream is collecting data? It seems to collect at roughly ~1-2 second intervals and return them to the generator.

issue_title: Compiling NotoSansArabicUI fails because of feaLib
issue_body:
This is really a ufo2ft and/or fontTools.feaLib issue, but I'm not sure where this should be handled since it seems to rely on Glyphs-specific behavior. 

What happens is that there are non-exporting glyphs in the font that have anchors on them. These are added to the UFO, but since they are not exported the anchors should be ignored when building the GPOS. Since they are read by feaLib there are conflicts like this:

`fontTools.feaLib.error.FeatureLibError: <features>:1956:9: Glyph twodotsverticalabove-ar.small cannot be in both @MC_top and @MC_center`

This glyph is only used as a component so isn't exported, but it has some anchors on it (here it has both a 'top' and a 'center' anchor). In this particular case those anchors are totally pointless, but in many cases non-exporting glyphs might have anchors that "shine through" in Glyphs app so might be needed. But these should never be put in the GPOS for glyphs that will not export.

issue_title: maxZones in static TTF files from pipeline has incorrect value
issue_body:
All TTF files we produce have incorrect value for maxZones. This information is reported by ftxvalidator running on Mac

There's just a single error for all files:
maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2


_ftxvalidator -t all -T \<font-file-name\>_

NotoSerifSinhala-SemiBold.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifSinhala-Thin.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-Black.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-Bold.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-Condensed.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-CondensedBlack.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-CondensedBold.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-CondensedExtraBold.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-CondensedExtraLight.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2
NotoSerifThai-CondensedLight.ttf.txt:			maxp: The maxZones field is equal to 1; it should be equal to 2

@brawer : does it have anything to do with hinting?

It would be nice if fontmake could build color fonts.

Glyphs already supports color fonts, both as [vectors](https://glyphsapp.com/tutorials/creating-a-microsoft-color-font) and [images](https://glyphsapp.com/tutorials/creating-an-apple-color-font). Therefore, a glyphs file should contain all information needed to build color fonts in various formats.

Sadly, color font formats are very fragmented. As a general tool chain, I think fontmake should be agnostic about formats and try to support them all. (Of course, it would be nice if the industry could converge eventually). As an intermediate format, SVG might work because it can be easily converted into PDF, PNG, and JPEG images. Later in the pipeline, those images can then be packaged into [sbix](https://developer.apple.com/fonts/TrueType-Reference-Manual/RM06/Chap6sbix.html), [CBDT](https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/cbdt.htm), [COLR](https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/colr.htm), [CBDT/CBLC](https://github.com/googlei18n/noto-emoji/blob/master/third_party/color_emoji/emoji_builder.py), and similar tables depending on what format was requested.

issue_title: Unexpected processing order of received messages
issue_body:
First of all: I guess we have no bug here, but a behavior which might need some documentation (if not existing yet).

Consider the following case:
A client subscribes to a topic and calls a function. The result of the function is awaited.

Now the server first publishes something on the topic and instantly afterward returns the result of the function call.

What happens now on the client side is that first the subscription handler is fired (synchronously I guess) and then once the future is resolved the function call result is processed.

The behavior can be easily simulated:
#### Client:
```
async def onJoin(self, details):
    await self.subscribe(self.handle_change, 'changed')

    all_changes_future = self.call('all_changes')
    await self.call('make_change', 'a change')

    all_changes = await all_changes_future

    print('all_changes: ', all_changes)

def handle_change(self, change):
    logging.info('change# %s', change)
```

#### Server
```
@wamp.register('make_change')
def make_change(self, the_change):
    print('make_change')
    self.all_changes.append(the_change)

    self.publish('changed', the_change)

    return the_change

@wamp.register('all_changes')
def all_changes(self):
    print('all_changes')
    return self.all_changes
```

The server will print
> all_changes
> make_change

while we see
> change:  a change
> all_changes:  ['a change', 'a change']

on the client side.

I'd say technically this behavior is correct. It might be unexpected for a developer though.

When could this be a problem?
I noticed this in practice when I have a function to retrieve a current state of some objects and send around any changes as delta.
Now there is a possibility for a race at the moment the client registers.
The complete state might not have been handled yet while a subscription is already processed.

I can solve this problem by adding sequence numbers to all sent packages. I can then process the received deltas with a higher sequence number once the complete state is read.

issue_title: newapi / component API accept session_factory as a keyword
issue_body:
```
class Redacted(ApplicationSession):
    s = None
    protocol = None

if __name__ == '__main__':

    args = parser.parse_args()

    extra = vars(args)
    device = extra.pop('serialport')
    url = extra.pop('url')
    realm = extra.pop('realm')
    port = next(serialgrep(device))
    ### Inserted code goes here ###
    run(component)
```
Given the above code, adding the following code *should* work but doesn't: 
```
    component = Component(
        transports=url
        session_factory = Redacted
        realm=realm,
        extra=extra
    )
```

However, what works is: 
```
    component = Component(
        transports=url,
        realm=realm,
        extra=extra
    )
    component.session_factory = Redacted
```

I believe that the property `session_factory` should be added as a kwarg, and @meejah asked me to submit this issue

issue_title: FYI: PANDAS and 'autoload', and identified problem with numexpr 2.4.4 on Windows
issue_body:
I had a recent issue that took some time to track down. In case this group should experience it. The issues is now KNOWN to be numexpr 2.4.4 and on Windows only when using many cores, >16 math errors occur due to a race condition. Initially I thought it was PANDAS 0.17 since I was NOT loading numexpr for use. It actually took a while and many error sessions to isolate because the race condition was only occurring about 1 to 1.4% of the time using the exact same input data. The failure always occurred in the first two pandas dataframe rows, values are set to x.x-e315. I learned that PANDAS autoloads any modules they support and autoenables capabilities like numexpr on ordinary expression like df[t]+=1.0 .... So even if you are not crafting numexpr vector operation strings, it will try and succeed for your code.

I could not produce the error with <16 cores. Which made the investigation less than fun.

2.4.6 is out and 'should' correct the issue. I will test soon, in the meantime I dropped back to 2.4.3
FYI
https://github.com/pydata/numexpr/issues/188

* Run following pipelines 
```
remote:

          out-file: remote.zip

```

Currently `DSC::define` has two rules:

1. The right hand side of ":" can only contain module names, not other defined groups
2. The search query only works with those who are module groups, not pipelines or pipeline groups

Alternatively, we might want to change the behavior to:

1. Allow right hand side to be module names or other defined groups
2. Disallow `DSC::define` groups to be used in query
3. Add to `dscrutils` an option to define groups on the fly (this already exists in `dsc-query` just need to port the feature).

Or even,

1. Allow right hand side to be module names or other defined groups
2. Allow all groups to be querable under certain rules:
  - always use the last pipeline variable
  - always use the first pipeline parameter

There are pros and cons for each of the 3 approaches. I do not see a clear winner as of now. We can keep thinking about it and come up with some combined, hopefully better solutions.
